title: RFC: Digisign 4W - Spouse Signing

td.p: Reuse /v1/loans/latest to expose spouse signing info; extend BFI approved callback, digisign process, and OneKYC for spouse signing
Loan Borrower Agreement — the legal document that must be signed by borrower (and spouse for married 4W borrowers) before disbursement.
Digital signature process powered by Digisign.id. Uses OneKYC partner token for authentication. Involves document upload, signing, and callback flows.
Manual (physical) signing process outside the app, used as fallback when digisign is not available or KYC is rejected.
The VF API Provider service (this repository) — acts as the middle-layer between the GoPay app frontend and BFI lender.
The lending partner for vehicle financing. Provides loan approval, LBA generation, and disbursement.
KYC verification service keyed on gopayAccountId. Determines whether progressive or non-progressive KYC is required.
The process where a married 4W borrower's spouse signs the LBA via digisign, required by BFI compliance for 4W loans and top-ups.

p: Currently, digisign (digital LBA signing) is only available for 2W borrowers. For 4W borrowers, spouse signing is required by BFI when the borrower is married, so 4W borrowers must go through a manual wet-sign process. This creates friction: approximately 60% of digisign users complete signing within 30 minutes of LBA generation, a speed unattainable with wet sign.

Implementing digisign for 4W — including spouse signing — also unlocks 4W top-ups, since BFI compliance requires spouse signing as a prerequisite. The pool of users eligible for 4W top-ups is growing, making this a revenue-critical feature.

This RFC covers the backend changes needed in vehicle-finance-api-provider to support spouse data ingestion from BFI, spouse auto-whitelisting, spouse KYC, spouse digisign, and exposing spouse signing status to both spouse and borrower via the existing /v1/loans/latest API.
Source: PRD — Digisign 4W Spouse Signing
The PRD defines 4 main requirements (backend-relevant subset):

ol.li: Receive spouse data from BFI on the approved callback (full name, phone number, e-KTP, DOB, place of birth). If no spouse data sent → spouse signing not required.
Spouse signing entrypoint — auto-whitelist spouse by phone number; spouse sees signing entrypoint in the VF app; PN + WhatsApp reminders.
Spouse KYC — call OneKYC SDK for spouse; only allow LBA access after OneKYC passes.
Borrower status tracking — borrower sees spouse signing status (signed / not signed), expiry, spouse name and phone number.

p: Confirmed from PRD Q&A: Borrower and spouse signing can happen in parallel. Lender signing takes place after both borrower and spouse signing complete. Only 1 spouse per borrower. No spouse data changes once passed to MAB. One spouse correlated to multiple borrowers → FIFO entrypoint ordering.
Go-to-market: Percentage rollout to merchant + driver user groups first, then consumer.

The system is a Spring Boot 3.2 + WebFlux reactive backend (hexagonal architecture) that mediates between the GoPay app frontend and the BFI lending partner. The existing digisign flow covers only 2W borrowers; 4W loans bypass digisign entirely.

p.b: Loan submission → BFI approval → Digisign (2W only today):

ol.li: Borrower submits a loan application via POST /v1/loans. Spouse data is captured in the request (SubmitLoanCustomerSpouseDTO), validated (LoanValidator), persisted in customers_info.spouse_data (encrypted JSONB), and embedded in loan.metadata.customerSpouse for outbound BFI submission.

BFI reviews the application and sends an approved callback to POST /partners/bfi/v1/loans/callbacks with BFILoanDTO (status = APPROVED). The callback is handled by LoanStatusChangeService.processCallback → case APPROVED → LoanService.updateApprovedLoan → handleDigisignFeature.

handleDigisignFeature checks the isDigisignFeatureEnabled flag and whether BFI returned a document URL in vehicleAssets. If eligible, it triggers LBA document generation via Kafka (GetLBADocumentEvent), which asynchronously fetches the LBA from BFI, uploads to OSS, and sets LoanDocument.status = PENDING.

The borrower opens the VF app, which calls GET /v1/loans/latest → LoanService.getLatestLoan → LoanConverter.toLatestLoanDetailDto. The response (LoanDetailDTO) includes digisignStatus from the latest LoanDocument.
Borrower clicks digisign → POST /v1/loans/document/digisign → LoanDocumentService.initiateDocumentSigning → Kafka event with processType = BORROWER_SIGNING.
DocumentSigningService.processDocumentSigning creates a DigisignProcess record (type = BORROWER_SIGNING), uploads the document to Digisign, and returns a signing URL to the frontend.

Digisign calls back POST /partners/digisign/v1/borrower/callback → DocumentSigningService.handleBorrowerCallback. On COMPLETED + VALID, the process status is set to SIGNED, and a new DocumentSigningRequestEvent with processType = LENDER_SIGNING is emitted automatically.
After lender signing completes (handleLenderCallback), the signed document is fetched from Digisign, uploaded to OSS, sent to BFI, and the loan status advances to ESIGNED.

p: 4W (CAR) loans today: The same flow exists, but handleDigisignFeature skips LBA generation for 4W repeat orders when is-4w-repeat-order-without-digisign is enabled. There is no spouse signing support — spouse data is collected at submission for BFI outbound only, and never used for digisign.

ul.li: No inbound spouse data from BFI: BFILoanDTO has no spouse field. BFI cannot send spouse signing data on the approved callback today.
No spouse signing in digisign: DigisignProcessType only has BORROWER_SIGNING and LENDER_SIGNING. No SPOUSE_SIGNING type, no spouse callback endpoint, no spouse partner config in DigisignClient.

No spouse whitelist: Existing customer_whitelist is keyed on gojek_customer_id and is staging-only feature gating. Cannot whitelist a spouse by phone number (the spouse may not have a gopay account yet at whitelist time).

No spouse info in /v1/loans/latest: LoanDetailDTO has no spouse fields. LoanConverter.toLatestLoanDetailDto does not map spouse data. The CustomersInfoDTO.spouseDataDTO is loaded internally but only used for address resolution.

No loan ↔ spouse KYC linkage: OneKYC is keyed on gopayAccountId. When the spouse completes KYC via OnekycController.oneKycCallback, there is no way to link the spouse's KYC result back to a specific loan to trigger spouse digisign.
No spouse digisign sequencing: handleBorrowerCallback auto-triggers LENDER_SIGNING after borrower signs. There is no hook to wait for spouse signing before lender signing.

td.p: GET /v1/loans/latest returns LoanDetailDTO — no spouse fields
toLatestLoanDetailDto builds response; spouse data not mapped
POST /partners/bfi/v1/loans/callbacks receives BFILoanDTO — no spouse field
LoanCustomerSpouseDTO: fullName, ktpNumber, birthDate, birthPlaceCode, birthPlaceName, phoneNumber
Encrypted JSONB in customers_info.spouse_data; embedded in loan.metadata.customerSpouse
On borrower SIGNED, auto-emits LENDER_SIGNING event
Two partner configs: borrower + lender. No spouse partner.
digisign_processes — process_type is TEXT, no migration needed for new enum value
Loan (1) → LoanDocument (latest) → DigisignProcesses (1-to-many by process type)
Keyed on gojek_customer_id, staging-only — not reusable for spouse-by-phone
Keyed on gopayAccountId; callback via OnekycController
4W loans are CAR productId="1"; 4W repeat orders can skip digisign via flag

p: After this RFC is implemented:

ul.li: BFI approved callback carries spouse data (when spouse signing is required). MAB creates a spouse whitelist entry by phone number, and generates/retrieves the spouse LBA document. Spouse signing data is not persisted in a new table — it is forwarded to Digisign for signing.

/v1/loans/latest returns spouse signing info in LoanDetailDTO — both for the spouse (to see the signing entrypoint with borrower name hash + loan expiry) and for the borrower (to see spouse signing status, expiry, spouse name + phone number).

Digisign supports a third process type SPOUSE_SIGNING. The spouse initiates signing via POST /v1/loans/document/digisign (extended with a process type parameter or a spouse-specific path). The spouse digisign callback is handled, and lender signing is only triggered after both borrower and spouse signing are complete.
OneKYC works as-is for the spouse's own gopay account. Spouse KYC result is linked back to the loan via spouse_whitelist (phone number → loan mapping) — no separate linkage table needed.
Notifications (PN + WhatsApp) are sent to the spouse when signing is required and daily from D+1 at 10 AM.
Feature flag gates the entire spouse signing flow, with percentage rollout to merchant + driver first.

callout: The API strategy is confirmed by the requester. Other decisions marked as TODO require discussion before pushing. Do not treat TODO items as decided.

td.p: CONFIRMED: Reuse GET /v1/loans/latest — extend LoanDetailDTO with a new spouseSigningInfo field. No new endpoint for spouse info retrieval.
Requester instruction: "reuse the api /v1/loans/latest to get the information for spouse"

BFI is the source of spouse signing requirement and spouse data (via approved callback). MAB does NOT persist spouse signing data in a new table — spouse data is forwarded to Digisign (3rd party) for signing. Spouse data for display comes from existing loan.metadata.customerSpouse (set at submission time). Signing status is tracked via spouse_whitelist.status (state machine) and digisign_processes (with SPOUSE_SIGNING type). OneKYC is the source of spouse KYC status.

BFI approved callback (sync HTTP) → persist spouse data + whitelist (sync DB write) → Kafka event for spouse LBA generation (async) → spouse opens app → /v1/loans/latest (sync read) → spouse KYC (sync via OneKYC SDK) → OneKYC callback (async Kafka) → spouse digisign initiation (sync HTTP) → Digisign callback (sync HTTP) → lender signing trigger (async Kafka).
CONFIRMED from PRD Q&A: Borrower and spouse signing can happen in parallel. Lender signing takes place after BOTH borrower and spouse signing are complete.
PRD Q&A #1 ("Can be in parallel") and Q&A #3 ("Lender signing will take place after borrower and spouse signing")
TODO: Confirm whether spouse whitelist lookup needs caching (phone number → loan/customer mapping). If yes, define key/value, TTL, invalidation on signing completion.

Partially confirmed from PRD: Percentage rollout to merchant + driver user groups first, then consumer. TODO: Confirm exact feature flag name, rollout percentages, default state (off), monitoring gate, and kill-switch mechanism.
PRD Go-to-market section; TODO: flag name + percentages

TODO: Confirm whether existing 4W loans in the pipeline (already approved but not yet signed) need spouse signing retroactively, or only new loans from feature-flag-on date are affected. No backfill for historical disbursed loans.

TODO: Confirm timeout/retry for spouse digisign initiation, duplicate BFI callback idempotency (PRD line 124 struck through re-hit capability — current scope does NOT support re-hits), and partial-success handling (e.g. spouse data persisted but LBA generation fails).

TODO: Confirm exact JSON contract with BFI — field names, nesting (inside customer vs separate spouse object), and whether a boolean isSpouseSigningRequired flag is sent or inferred from presence of spouse data.
CONFIRMED: Spouse signs a separate LBA document (separate LoanDocument row). BFI generates LBA for both borrower and spouse.
Requester confirmation + PRD "generate LBA for borrower and spouse"

CONFIRMED: No new spouse_signing table. Spouse signing data is NOT persisted in MAB — forwarded to Digisign (3rd party). Spouse data for display from existing loan.metadata.customerSpouse. Signing status tracked via spouse_whitelist.status + digisign_processes (SPOUSE_SIGNING type).
TODO: Confirm how spouse's phone number (from BFI) is matched to a gopay account when the spouse opens the VF app. Is there an existing phone-to-gopayAccountId lookup service?
TODO: Confirm whether BFI/Digisign provides a separate signature partner config for spouse, or whether the borrower partner config is reused with a different submission type.

ul.li: Extend BFI approved callback (BFILoanDTO) to receive spouse data from BFI.
Persist spouse signing data and auto-whitelist spouse by phone number on approved callback.
Extend /v1/loans/latest (LoanDetailDTO) to expose spouse signing info to both spouse and borrower.
Add DigisignProcessType.SPOUSE_SIGNING and spouse digisign flow (initiation, callback, sequencing).
Link spouse OneKYC result back to the loan to trigger spouse digisign.
Send PN and WhatsApp notifications to the spouse (signing required + daily reminders).
Feature flag for spouse signing with percentage rollout.
Backend scope only — 4W (CAR) product type.
Frontend UI changes (bottom sheet, floating action button, digisign preview UI) — handled by frontend team.
2W spouse signing — 4W only per PRD.
BFI re-hit approved callback to update spouse data (struck through in PRD — not supported in current scope).
Spouse signing from the borrower app (regulatory issue per PRD Q&A).
Wet sign fallback implementation details (PRD mentions it but details TBD).

p: The solution reuses the existing /v1/loans/latest API as the single data surface for spouse signing info, and extends three existing integration points (BFI callback, Digisign, OneKYC) to support the spouse actor. No new top-level API endpoint is introduced.

Ownership & data flow: MAB (vehicle-finance-api-provider) orchestrates the spouse signing flow but does NOT persist spouse signing data in a new table. BFI pushes spouse data via the approved callback (sync HTTP). MAB creates a spouse whitelist entry (by phone number → loan_id), and triggers spouse LBA generation via Kafka. Spouse signing data is forwarded to Digisign (3rd party) for signing. When the spouse opens the VF app, the app calls /v1/loans/latest — MAB resolves the spouse's gopay account to a pending spouse signing record via the whitelist (phone number → loan mapping) and returns spouse signing info (display data from loan.metadata.customerSpouse, status from spouse_whitelist.status). The spouse then goes through OneKYC (using their own gopay account), and upon KYC approval, MAB updates the whitelist status and allows spouse digisign initiation.

ol.li: NOT_REQUIRED — BFI does not send spouse data on approved callback.
PENDING_KYC — spouse data received, whitelist created, spouse LBA generated. Waiting for spouse to open app + complete KYC.
KYC_PASSED — spouse OneKYC approved. Spouse can initiate digisign.
SIGNING_IN_PROGRESS — spouse digisign initiated, document uploaded to Digisign.
SIGNED — spouse digisign callback received with COMPLETED + VALID.
FAILED — spouse digisign callback received with ERROR, or KYC rejected.
EXPIRED — LBA expiration time passed before spouse signed.

p: Sync/async boundaries: BFI callback and Digisign callbacks are sync HTTP. LBA generation, lender signing trigger, and notifications are async via Kafka. OneKYC callback is async via Kafka. Spouse whitelist lookup and /v1/loans/latest are sync reads.

Signing sequence: Borrower and spouse signing can proceed in parallel. The existing handleBorrowerCallback auto-trigger of LENDER_SIGNING must be modified to wait for spouse signing completion (if spouse signing is required) before emitting the lender signing event. If spouse signing is not required, the flow remains unchanged.

Failure handling: BFI callback idempotency on partnerSubmissionId (no re-hits in current scope). Spouse digisign failures are retried via the existing retryStuckDocumentSigning pattern. KYC rejection sets spouse signing status to FAILED and the loan falls back to wet sign (details TODO).

What is intentionally out of path: No separate spouse API endpoint. No changes to the OneKYC service itself (spouse uses the same OneKYC flow with their own gopay account). No changes to the existing 2W digisign flow.
h1: User Story: US-001 — BFI Approved Callback: Receive & Persist Spouse Data + Auto-Whitelist
p: As MAB (VF API Provider), I want to receive spouse data from BFI on the approved callback and auto-whitelist the spouse by phone number, so that the spouse signing flow can be triggered when the spouse opens the VF app.

td.p: BFI sends approved callback with spouse data (fullName, phoneNumber, ktpNumber, birthDate, birthPlace)
MAB parses spouse data, persists it, and sets spouse signing status to PENDING_KYC
BFI sends approved callback without spouse data
MAB sets spouse signing status to NOT_REQUIRED and proceeds with borrower-only digisign
Spouse data is received from BFI
A spouse whitelist entry is created keyed on spouse phone number, linking to the loan ID and borrower customer ID
Spouse data is received and loan is 4W (CAR)
Spouse LBA generation is triggered via Kafka event (same as borrower LBA, but flagged for spouse)
Spouse data is received and loan is 2W (MOTORCYCLE)
Spouse signing is ignored — spouse signing is 4W only
BFI sends duplicate approved callback with same partnerSubmissionId
MAB is idempotent — does not create duplicate whitelist or spouse data. PRD does not support re-hits to update spouse data.

p: Extend BFILoanDTO with a new BFILoanSpouseDTO record (fullName, phoneNumber, ktpNumber, birthDate, birthPlace) and an optional isSpouseSigningRequired flag (or infer from presence of spouse data — TODO pending BFI contract confirmation). In LoanStatusChangeService.processCallback case APPROVED, pass spouse data to LoanService.updateApprovedLoan. In handleDigisignFeature, after borrower LBA generation, add a branch: if spouse data is present and loan is ProductType.CAR, persist spouse signing data (extend LoanMetadataDTO or create a new spouse_signing table), create a spouse whitelist entry, and trigger spouse LBA generation.
New migration: spouse_whitelist table (phone_number, loan_id, customer_id, status, created_at, updated_at) and optionally spouse_signing table or extend loan.metadata with spouse signing fields.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant BFI participant BFIController as BFIController participant StatusService as LoanStatusChangeService participant LoanService as LoanService participant DB as Database participant Kafka as Kafka BFI->>BFIController: POST /partners/bfi/v1/loans/callbacks (BFILoanDTO with spouse) BFIController->>StatusService: processCallback(request) StatusService->>StatusService: case APPROVED StatusService->>LoanService: updateApprovedLoan(request, VERIFIED, agreementNo) LoanService->>LoanService: handleDigisignFeature(request, dto) LoanService->>DB: Persist spouse signing data (status=PENDING_KYC) LoanService->>DB: Create spouse_whitelist (phone_number, loan_id, customer_id) LoanService->>Kafka: Emit GetSpouseLBADocumentEvent(loanId) LoanService-->>StatusService: Updated loan DTO StatusService-->>BFIController: Success BFIController-->>BFI: 200 OK

td.p: Spouse signing data is NOT persisted in MAB — forwarded to Digisign. Spouse data for display comes from existing loan.metadata.customerSpouse. Signing status tracked via spouse_whitelist.status + digisign_processes (SPOUSE_SIGNING type).

p: Owner / Caller: BFI (caller) → MAB BFIController (owner)
Auth Boundary: BFI JWT + ECDSA payload signing (existing BFIPayloadEncryptionPort)
Idempotency: Idempotent on partnerSubmissionId. Duplicate callbacks do not create duplicate whitelist or spouse data. PRD does not support re-hits to update spouse data.
Timeout / Retry: Existing BFI callback timeout/retry policy. MAB returns 200 OK if processing is accepted.
Compatibility: Backward compatible — spouse fields are optional. Existing callbacks without spouse data continue to work.

p.b: Request (extended payload — TODO: field names pending BFI confirmation):

p: Success Response: Existing BFILoanCallbackResponseDTO — unchanged.

Error Semantics: Existing error handling via GlobalExceptionHandler. New error code SPOUSE_DATA_INVALID for malformed spouse data (non-blocking — logged and spouse signing set to NOT_REQUIRED if spouse data is present but invalid).
h1: User Story: US-002 — Expose Spouse Signing Info to Spouse via /v1/loans/latest
p: As a spouse, I want to see the LBA signing entrypoint when I open the VF app, so that I can sign the LBA to help my spouse get the loan.

td.p: Spouse is whitelisted (phone number matches a gopay account) and has a pending spouse signing
Spouse opens VF app, app calls GET /v1/loans/latest with spouse's x-id
Response includes spouseSigningInfo with borrower name (hashed, e.g. a***i s***o), loan expiry time, and signing status PENDING_KYC
Spouse is not whitelisted or has no pending spouse signing
Spouse opens VF app, app calls GET /v1/loans/latest
Response does not include spouseSigningInfo (or includes it as null) — no signing entrypoint shown
Response includes spouseSigningInfo with status SIGNED — no signing entrypoint shown
One spouse is correlated to multiple borrowers (FIFO)
Response returns the earliest pending spouse signing first (FIFO ordering by created_at)
Response includes spouseSigningInfo with status FAILED — signing entrypoint not shown (Variant B: bottom sheet not triggered)

p: This is the confirmed reuse approach. Add a new SpouseSigningInfoDTO to LoanDetailDTO. In LoanService.getLatestLoan, after fetching the latest loan, check if the caller is a spouse by looking up spouse_whitelist by the caller's phone number (resolved from their gopay account — TODO: confirm phone-to-gopay lookup). If found, build SpouseSigningInfoDTO with: borrower name (hashed via textUtil), loan expiry, signing status. Map it in LoanConverter.toLatestLoanDetailDto.

If the caller is the borrower (not the spouse), the same spouseSigningInfo field is populated with the spouse's signing status, spouse name, and spouse phone number (for borrower tracking — see US-004).

The spouse_whitelist lookup is the key new read path. The caller identity (x-id header = customerId) must be resolved to a phone number to match the whitelist. TODO: confirm if there is an existing phone-to-customerId lookup, or if a new lookup is needed.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant SpouseApp as Spouse App participant LoanController as LoanController participant LoanService as LoanService participant WhitelistDB as spouse_whitelist (DB) participant LoanConverter as LoanConverter SpouseApp->>LoanController: GET /v1/loans/latest (x-id: spouseCustomerId) LoanController->>LoanService: getLatestLoan(customerId) LoanService->>LoanService: Resolve phone number from customerId LoanService->>WhitelistDB: findByPhoneNumber(spousePhoneNumber) ORDER BY created_at ASC WhitelistDB-->>LoanService: Spouse whitelist entry (FIFO) LoanService->>LoanService: Fetch loan + spouse signing status LoanService->>LoanConverter: toLatestLoanDetailDto(loan, spouseSigningInfo) LoanConverter-->>LoanService: LoanDetailDTO with spouseSigningInfo LoanService-->>LoanController: LoanDetailDTO LoanController-->>SpouseApp: 200 OK BaseResponse<LoanDetailDTO>
p: Owner / Caller: VF API Provider (owner) → GoPay App (caller, both borrower and spouse)
Auth Boundary: x-id header (customer ID). Existing auth — no change.
Idempotency: Read-only. No change.
Timeout / Retry: Existing timeout. Spouse whitelist lookup adds one DB read — latency impact minimal (indexed on phone_number PK).
Compatibility: Backward compatible — spouseSigningInfo is a new optional field. Existing clients that do not read it are unaffected.
Request: No change — GET /v1/loans/latest with x-id header.

p.b: For borrower (includes spouse details for tracking):

p: Error Response: Existing error handling. No new error codes for this endpoint.
Error Semantics: If spouse whitelist lookup fails (DB error), spouseSigningInfo is set to null and the response still succeeds — spouse signing entrypoint is not shown. Logged as warning.

td.p: New field — spouse signing info for both borrower and spouse views

h1: User Story: US-003 — Spouse KYC Verification & Spouse Digisign Initiation
p: As MAB, I want to call OneKYC for the spouse and initiate spouse digisign after KYC passes, so that the spouse can sign the LBA digitally and securely.

td.p: Spouse has PENDING_KYC status and clicks the signing entrypoint
Frontend calls OneKYC SDK for the spouse's gopay account
OneKYC determines the KYC process (progressive/non-progressive/exempt). Existing OneKYC flow is reused as-is.
Spouse OneKYC callback is received with APPROVED status
MAB links the spouse's gopayAccountId to the loan via the loan-spouse KYC linkage, updates spouse signing status to KYC_PASSED
Spouse has KYC_PASSED status and clicks digisign
Frontend calls POST /v1/loans/document/digisign (extended with spouse process type)
MAB creates a DigisignProcess with type SPOUSE_SIGNING, uploads spouse LBA to Digisign, returns signing URL
MAB validates COMPLETED + VALID, updates process to SIGNED, updates spouse signing status to SIGNED
Both borrower and spouse signing are SIGNED
Spouse digisign callback is processed (or borrower callback is processed if spouse already signed)
MAB emits LENDER_SIGNING event — lender signing is triggered only after both borrower and spouse are signed
Spouse OneKYC callback is received with REJECTED status
MAB updates spouse signing status to FAILED. Loan falls back to wet sign for both borrower and spouse (details TODO).
Spouse signing is SIGNED but borrower has not signed yet
MAB does NOT trigger lender signing yet — waits for borrower to sign

p: Three changes are needed:

1. Spouse KYC linkage via spouse_whitelist: No separate loan_spouse_kyc table. When the spouse completes OneKYC, ApprovedStatusHandler is extended to check if the gopayAccountId belongs to a spouse (resolve gopayAccountId → phone number → lookup spouse_whitelist by phone number). If found, update spouse_whitelist.status = KYC_PASSED. The whitelist entry already links phone number → loan_id, so no separate linkage table is needed.

2. Spouse digisign initiation: Extend LoanDocumentService.initiateDocumentSigning to accept a process type parameter (or add a separate initiateSpouseDocumentSigning method). Add DigisignProcessType.SPOUSE_SIGNING to the enum. In DocumentSigningService.createSubmitDocumentSigningRequest, add a SPOUSE_SIGNING branch that uses the spouse's gopay account and the spouse LBA document. In DigisignClient, add a third partner config (vf-spouse-signature-partner — TODO: confirm with BFI/Digisign) and spouse submission type.

3. Spouse digisign callback + lender sequencing: Add @PostMapping("/spouse/callback") to DigisignCallbackController → DocumentSigningService.handleSpouseCallback. On COMPLETED + VALID, update spouse signing status to SIGNED. Then check if borrower is also SIGNED — if yes, emit LENDER_SIGNING event. Modify handleBorrowerCallback similarly: after borrower SIGNED, check if spouse signing is required and if spouse is SIGNED — only then emit LENDER_SIGNING.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant SpouseApp as Spouse App participant OneKYC as OneKYC SDK participant OnekycCtrl as OnekycController participant KYCHandler as ApprovedStatusHandler participant WhitelistDB as spouse_whitelist (DB) participant DocCtrl as LoanDocumentController participant DocSignService as DocumentSigningService participant Digisign as DigisignClient participant DigisignCB as DigisignCallbackController SpouseApp->>OneKYC: Complete KYC (spouse gopayAccountId) OneKYC->>OnekycCtrl: PUT /partners/onekyc/v1/callback (APPROVED) OnekycCtrl->>KYCHandler: processOnekycCallbackReceivedEvent KYCHandler->>WhitelistDB: Lookup spouse by gopayAccountId → phone number WhitelistDB-->>KYCHandler: Spouse whitelist entry found KYCHandler->>WhitelistDB: Update status = KYC_PASSED SpouseApp->>DocCtrl: POST /v1/loans/document/digisign (spouse) DocCtrl->>DocSignService: initiateSpouseDocumentSigning(loanId, deviceInfo) DocSignService->>DocSignService: Create DigisignProcess (SPOUSE_SIGNING) DocSignService->>Digisign: Upload spouse LBA + submit signing request Digisign-->>DocSignService: Signing URL DocSignService-->>DocCtrl: Signing URL DocCtrl-->>SpouseApp: 200 OK (signing URL) Note over SpouseApp,Digisign: Spouse signs in Digisign app Digisign->>DigisignCB: POST /partners/digisign/v1/spouse/callback (COMPLETED) DigisignCB->>DocSignService: handleSpouseCallback DocSignService->>DocSignService: Validate COMPLETED + VALID → SIGNED DocSignService->>DocSignService: Check if borrower also SIGNED? alt Borrower also SIGNED DocSignService->>DocSignService: Emit LENDER_SIGNING event else Borrower not yet SIGNED DocSignService->>DocSignService: Wait — do not trigger lender yet end

td.p: Updated to KYC_PASSED when spouse OneKYC approved, FAILED when rejected
New value SPOUSE_SIGNING added to enum (TEXT column, no migration)

p: Owner / Caller: VF API Provider (owner) → GoPay App (caller)
Auth Boundary: x-id header (spouse's customer ID). Spouse must have KYC_PASSED status.
Idempotency: If spouse digisign is already initiated (process exists with type SPOUSE_SIGNING and status != FAILED), return the existing signing URL.
Timeout / Retry: Existing digisign timeout. Retry via retryStuckDocumentSigning extended for spouse.
Compatibility: Backward compatible — existing borrower digisign requests are unaffected. The spouse path is triggered by the caller being a whitelisted spouse with KYC_PASSED status.
Request: Existing InitiateDocumentSubmissionRequestDTO — no change needed. The system determines spouse vs borrower from the caller identity + whitelist.
Success Response: Existing response with signing URL — no change.
Owner / Caller: Digisign (caller) → MAB DigisignCallbackController (owner)
Auth Boundary: OneKYC partner token validation (existing pattern)
Idempotency: Idempotent on submissionId. Duplicate callbacks do not trigger lender signing twice.
Timeout / Retry: Digisign retries on non-2xx response. MAB processes synchronously.
Compatibility: New endpoint — no backward compatibility concern.
Request: Existing ESignDocumentCallbackRequest — no change.
Success Response: 200 OK — existing pattern.
Error Semantics: 400 for malformed request, 404 for unknown submission ID, 5xx for processing failure (Digisign will retry).

td.p: New process type SPOUSE_SIGNING in event payload
Extended to handle spouse signing completion + lender signing gate check

h1: User Story: US-004 — Expose Spouse Signing Status to Borrower via /v1/loans/latest
p: As a borrower, I want to track my spouse's digisign status, so that I can follow up with my spouse if they haven't signed yet.

td.p: Borrower has a 4W loan with spouse signing required (status = PENDING_KYC or SIGNING_IN_PROGRESS)
Borrower opens VF app, app calls GET /v1/loans/latest with borrower's x-id
Response includes spouseSigningInfo with spouse signing status, spouse name, spouse phone number, and loan expiry date
Spouse signing is not required (status = NOT_REQUIRED)
Response does not include spouseSigningInfo (or includes it with status NOT_REQUIRED)
Both borrower and spouse have signed
Borrower can proceed to the loan processing page — both signing statuses are complete

p: This story reuses the same spouseSigningInfo field added in US-002, but populated for the borrower view. In LoanConverter.toLatestLoanDetailDto, when the caller is the borrower (identified by loan.customerId == x-id), populate spouseSigningInfo with: spouse signing status (from spouse_signing table or loan.metadata), spouse name (decrypted from customers_info.spouse_data), spouse phone number (decrypted), and loan expiry date. The borrower name hash is not needed for the borrower view.

The key difference from US-002: for the spouse view, borrowerNameHashed is populated and spouse name/phone are null (privacy). For the borrower view, spouse name/phone are populated and borrowerNameHashed is null.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant BorrowerApp as Borrower App participant LoanController as LoanController participant LoanService as LoanService participant DB as Database (spouse_signing + customers_info) participant LoanConverter as LoanConverter BorrowerApp->>LoanController: GET /v1/loans/latest (x-id: borrowerCustomerId) LoanController->>LoanService: getLatestLoan(borrowerCustomerId) LoanService->>LoanService: Fetch latest loan (existing flow) LoanService->>DB: Load spouse signing status + spouse data (encrypted) DB-->>LoanService: Spouse signing info (status, name, phone) LoanService->>LoanConverter: toLatestLoanDetailDto(loan, spouseSigningInfo) LoanConverter-->>LoanService: LoanDetailDTO with spouseSigningInfo (borrower view) LoanService-->>LoanController: LoanDetailDTO LoanController-->>BorrowerApp: 200 OK BaseResponse<LoanDetailDTO>
p: Same endpoint as US-002 — GET /v1/loans/latest. The response shape is identical; only the populated fields differ based on caller identity (borrower vs spouse). See US-002 API contract for the response schema.
h1: User Story: US-005 — Spouse Signing Reminder Notifications
p: As a spouse, I want to receive push notifications and WhatsApp messages reminding me to sign the LBA, so that I don't forget to complete the signing.

td.p: Spouse data is received from BFI approved callback and spouse signing is PENDING_KYC
MAB sends an initial PN (title: "Tandatangan Dulu, Baru Cair!") and WhatsApp message to the spouse's phone number
Spouse signing is still pending (not SIGNED)
D+1 at 10 AM from the approved callback date (and daily thereafter)
MAB sends a daily reminder PN (title: "Kamu ditungguin pasangan kamu nih...") and WhatsApp message
Spouse signing is completed (SIGNED) or expired (EXPIRED)
No reminder is sent — reminders stop once signed or expired
TODO: WhatsApp content and CTA deeplink are TBD per PRD — confirm with PM

p: Two notification triggers:

1. Initial notification (on approved callback): In LoanService.handleDigisignFeature, after spouse data is persisted and whitelist is created, emit a SpouseSigningRequiredEvent Kafka event. The notification service (existing pattern) consumes this and sends PN + WhatsApp to the spouse's phone number.

2. Daily reminders (D+1 at 10 AM): Add a new XXL-Job (@XxlJob("spouseSigningReminderJob")) in domain/xxljob/ that queries all spouse_whitelist entries with status in (PENDING_KYC, KYC_PASSED, SIGNING_IN_PROGRESS) and created_at < now - 1 day, and sends the daily reminder PN + WhatsApp. The job runs at 10 AM Jakarta time.
PN and WhatsApp templates are configured in application.yaml following the existing notification config pattern (see existing gofin.vfs.notifications.whatsapp and PN template keys).
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant BFI as BFI participant LoanService as LoanService participant Kafka as Kafka participant NotifService as Notification Service participant XXLJob as XXL-Job Scheduler participant Spouse as Spouse (PN/WA) BFI->>LoanService: Approved callback with spouse data LoanService->>Kafka: SpouseSigningRequiredEvent(loanId, phone, borrowerName) Kafka->>NotifService: Consume event NotifService->>Spouse: PN "Tandatangan Dulu, Baru Cair!" + WA (initial) Note over XXLJob,Spouse: D+1 at 10 AM Jakarta time, daily XXLJob->>LoanService: spouseSigningReminderJob (query pending spouse signings) LoanService-->>XXLJob: List of pending spouse signings XXLJob->>NotifService: Send daily reminder for each pending spouse NotifService->>Spouse: PN "Kamu ditungguin pasangan kamu nih..." + WA (daily)

ul.li: BFI: Must extend approved callback to include spouse data. Must confirm payload contract and whether a separate spouse LBA is generated. TODO: BFI contract confirmation needed before implementation.
Digisign: Must confirm spouse signature partner config (or confirm borrower partner can be reused). TODO: Digisign partner confirmation needed.

OneKYC: No changes needed — spouse uses existing OneKYC flow with their own gopay account. However, the linkage between spouse KYC result and the loan requires a new lookup (phone → whitelist → loan).
Platform team: Must confirm phone-to-gopayAccountId lookup service for spouse whitelist resolution. TODO.
Frontend team: Must implement UI for spouse signing entrypoint, digisign preview, and borrower status tracking. Backend provides data via /v1/loans/latest.
XXL-Job scheduler: Must be available for daily reminder job. Already used in the codebase.

td.p: Create Flyway migration: spouse_whitelist table (phone_number PK, loan_id, customer_id, status, created_at, updated_at). No spouse_signing or loan_spouse_kyc tables — spouse signing data forwarded to Digisign, not persisted in MAB.
Extend BFILoanDTO with BFILoanSpouseDTO + parse in LoanStatusChangeService case APPROVED
Create spouse whitelist entry in LoanService.handleDigisignFeature + forward spouse data to Digisign for LBA generation
Trigger spouse LBA generation via Kafka event + extend LBADocumentService for spouse LBA (separate LoanDocument row)
Add SpouseSigningInfoDTO to LoanDetailDTO + map in LoanConverter.toLatestLoanDetailDto
Implement spouse whitelist lookup in LoanService.getLatestLoan (phone → whitelist → loan) + FIFO ordering
Add DigisignProcessType.SPOUSE_SIGNING enum + extend DocumentSigningService for spouse initiation
Extend DigisignClient with spouse partner config + spouse submission type
Modify handleBorrowerCallback + handleSpouseCallback to gate LENDER_SIGNING on both borrower + spouse signed
Extend ApprovedStatusHandler to link spouse KYC result via spouse_whitelist (resolve gopayAccountId → phone → whitelist → update status = KYC_PASSED)
Add spouse signing notification events (initial + daily reminder XXL-Job at 10 AM Jakarta)
Add feature flag is-spouse-signing-enabled + 4W (CAR) gating + user group percentage rollout
Unit tests for all new services, converters, and callback handlers
Integration tests for BFI callback with spouse data, spouse digisign flow, lender signing gate
Confirm BFI callback payload contract + spouse LBA document separation
Confirm Digisign spouse partner config + phone-to-gopayAccountId lookup
Non-Functional Requirements — latency SLA, throughput, error rate targets
Spouse whitelist lookup adds one indexed DB read to /v1/loans/latest. Latency impact < 10ms expected. Monitor p99.
Security — authentication, authorisation, data access control
Spouse accesses /v1/loans/latest with their own x-id. Spouse must only see borrower name hash, not full PII. Borrower sees spouse name + phone (decrypted via existing EncryptionUtil pattern).
Data Privacy / PII — new PII fields, data masking, retention policy
Spouse data (KTP, phone, DOB) is PII — must be encrypted at rest using existing EncryptionUtil (AES) in converters. Borrower name hash in spouse view must not reveal full name.
Spouse signing is required by BFI compliance for 4W loans. Regulatory requirement: spouse cannot sign from borrower app (per PRD Q&A).
Risk & Fraud — new attack surface, fraud rule changes, risk signal impact
Spouse whitelist by phone number — ensure phone number validation. Prevent whitelist injection. TODO: fraud review.
Customer Support (CS) Escalation — new error codes, CS tooling, escalation playbook
New error codes: SPOUSE_DATA_INVALID, SPOUSE_SIGNING_EXPIRED, SPOUSE_KYC_REJECTED. CS must be able to check spouse signing status.
Backward Compatibility — breaking API changes, client migration plan
All changes are backward compatible — spouseSigningInfo is a new optional field. BFI callback spouse fields are optional. Digisign spouse callback is a new endpoint.
Feature Flag / Kill Switch — mechanism to enable/disable safely without redeploy
Feature flag is-spouse-signing-enabled (default: false). Gates BFI spouse data parsing, whitelist creation, spouse digisign, and notifications. Kill switch = set flag to false.
Observability — new metrics, dashboards, alerting rules

New metrics: spouse_signing_whitelist_created, spouse_signing_kyc_passed, spouse_signing_completed, spouse_signing_failed, spouse_signing_expired. Tag with USER_GROUP. Dashboard for funnel conversion.
Rate Limiting & Throttling — abuse prevention, quota impact on new endpoints
No new public endpoints — spouse callback is partner-only (Digisign). /v1/loans/latest already rate-limited.
Third-party / External Dependencies — vendor SLA, fallback if external system is down

BFI, Digisign, OneKYC are existing dependencies. If BFI does not send spouse data, spouse signing is not required (graceful degradation). If Digisign is down for spouse, existing retry pattern applies.
Testing Strategy — unit, integration, E2E, regression, load testing coverage

Unit tests for new converters, services, callback handlers. Integration tests with WireMock for BFI callback + Digisign callback + OneKYC callback. E2E for full spouse signing flow. Regression: ensure 2W digisign and 4W borrower-only digisign are unaffected.

p: Feature flag: gofin.vfs.general.is-spouse-signing-enabled (default: false)

ol.li: Phase 1 — Merchant + Driver user groups (percentage rollout): Enable flag for MERCHANT and DRIVER user groups. Start with small percentage (e.g. 10%), monitor funnel and error rates for 3-5 days. Gradually increase to 100% for MERCHANT + DRIVER.
Phase 2 — Consumer user group: After Phase 1 is stable at 100%, enable for CONSUMER user group. Same gradual percentage rollout.

ul.li: Spouse signing funnel: whitelist created → KYC passed → digisign initiated → signed
Error rate: spouse_signing_failed metric < 5%
Latency: /v1/loans/latest p99 < existing baseline + 50ms
BFI callback success rate unchanged
Digisign callback success rate for spouse > 95%

p: Kill switch: Set is-spouse-signing-enabled = false. New BFI callbacks will not parse spouse data. Existing pending spouse signings remain in their current state (no new digisign initiations, no new reminders). Borrower-only digisign flow is unaffected.
TODO: Confirm exact rollout percentages and phase durations with PM.

ol.li: Immediate action: Set is-spouse-signing-enabled = false via config refresh (no redeploy needed if using dynamic config).
BFI callback: Spouse data in BFI callbacks will be ignored. Loans will proceed with borrower-only digisign (or wet sign for 4W if digisign was not previously enabled for 4W).
Existing pending spouse signings: Spouses who have already started KYC or digisign can complete the flow. No new spouse signings will be initiated.
Data safety: spouse_whitelist and spouse_signing tables remain — no data loss. Data can be used for re-enablement.
User-facing implications: Spouses will no longer see the signing entrypoint. Borrowers will no longer see spouse signing status. 4W loans revert to wet sign (existing behavior).
Notify: VF team, PM, BFI partner team, CS team.

p: TODO: Jot down the conclusion of the RFC review meeting discussion.

li.a: BFI One Pager — Spouse Digital Signature for 4W

ul.li: Repository: vehicle-finance-api-provider

checkbox:
BFI callback payload: What is the exact JSON contract for spouse data? Is a separate isSpouseSigningRequired flag sent, or inferred from presence of spouse data?
TODO: Confirm with BFI

Spouse LBA document: Does the spouse sign a separate LBA document (separate LoanDocument row) or the same LBA as the borrower?
TODO: Confirm with BFI — PRD says "generate LBA for borrower and spouse" implying separate documents

Spouse phone → gopay account matching: How is the spouse's phone number matched to a gopay account when the spouse opens the VF app?
TODO: Confirm with platform team — is there an existing phone-to-gopayAccountId lookup?

Spouse digisign partner config: Does BFI/Digisign provide a separate signature partner for spouse, or is the borrower partner reused?
TODO: Confirm with BFI/Digisign

Spouse KYC rejected: What is the exact backend behavior? Does the loan fall back to wet sign for both borrower and spouse?
TODO: Confirm with PM/BFI — PRD says "Wet Sign" but details TBD

Cache strategy: Does the spouse whitelist lookup need caching?
TODO: Discuss with team — if yes, define key/value, TTL, invalidation

Feature flag details: Exact flag name, rollout percentages, phase durations?
TODO: Confirm with PM

Backfill: Do existing 4W loans in the pipeline need spouse signing retroactively?
TODO: Confirm with PM

WhatsApp content and CTA deeplink: PN titles are defined but WA content is TBD
TODO: Confirm with PM

LBA expiration: Can borrower and spouse LBA have different expiration times?
TODO: Confirm with BFI — PRD says "aiming to be the same"

ul.li: TODO: add notes.
